hooks.command: if ! pgrep -f 'dashboard/server.py' > /dev/null 2>&1; then mkdir -p .claude/team && nohup uv run python "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/dashboard/server.py" --project-dir "$(pwd)" > .claude/team/dashboard.log 2>&1 & sleep 1; URL=$(grep -oP 'http://[^ ]+' .claude/team/dashboard.log 2>/dev/null | head -1); if [ -n "$URL" ]; then (xdg-open "$URL" 2>/dev/null || open "$URL" 2>/dev/null) & fi; fi

hooks.prompt: You are the session-manager agent. First, check if .claude/team/initialized exists. If it does NOT exist, run the First Run onboarding flow (see your instructions) before producing the briefing. If it does exist, skip onboarding and produce a session briefing by reading memory files, git status, open issues, and team state. Keep it under 20 lines.

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); AGENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.subagent_type // .tool_input.name // "agent"'); DESC=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.description // ""'); jq -nc --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" --arg detail "$AGENT: $DESC" '{ts:$ts,agent:"orchestrator",event:"spawn",detail:$detail}' >> .claude/team/activity.jsonl 2>/dev/null

INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '^git commit'; then if [ ! -f .claude/team/qa-approved ]; then echo '{"decision": "block", "reason": "QA Engineer has not approved. Run /team-review first."}'; else rm -f .claude/team/qa-approved; fi; fi
